methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $50M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 9 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

private.name: Hadrian
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1.6B

private.name: Nimble Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1B

data.narrative:
Simbe Robotics is a Series C robotics and manufacturing company that has raised $50M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparison universe of nine same-sector and adjacent-stage private companies, supplemented by one public comparable, providing a reasonably populated peer set for triangulation in the frontier robotics space.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression methodology, anchoring to the $50M total raised as a proxy baseline and applying a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical for Series C advancement, implies a valuation of $85M. Peer-median analysis across nine robotics and manufacturing firms at similar or proximate stages yields a median valuation of $1.6B, with a range spanning $300M (floor) to $10.0B (ceiling). The peer set includes companies such as Hadrian and Exotec at $1.6B and $2.0B respectively, as well as earlier-stage but substantially valued firms like 1X Technologies at $10.0B.

The two methods produce a wide implied range of $72M to $1.8B, with a median of $842M. The stage-progression method likely underweights recent sectoral capital flows and valuation momentum in frontier robotics, while the peer-median approach may reflect heterogeneity in business model maturity, customer traction, and technology differentiation across the nine comparables. The central uncertainty lies in Simbe's position within the peer cohort on revenue, retention, and path to profitability—data not available in this analysis.
